title: Grouped ranking model plus AUC-first OOF rank stacking
summary: ROC AUC rewards ordering, and CatBoost has grouped ranking/classification objectives designed for data with internal group structure.
rationale: ROC AUC rewards ordering, and CatBoost has grouped ranking/classification objectives designed for data with internal group structure. A stint naturally forms a group where only the last pre-stop lap should rank highest. Public docs also show AUC accepts decision values, so rank blending is more aligned than early probability calibration.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost grouped model with `group_id = Race_Year + Driver + Stint` using `QueryCrossEntropy` or a pairwise ranking objective, then blend its out-of-fold ranks with the best existing classifier using simple rank averaging or greedy stacking. Keep calibration optional and late; prioritize diversity and OOF correlation reduction over probability sharpness.
expected_effect: Can improve within-stint ordering of imminent-stop laps and add low-correlation signal to the current absolute-feature classifier.
risk: Bad group definitions or sparse positive groups can hurt. Ranking objectives may improve local ordering while degrading global discrimination if blended poorly.
